description: Webhook configuration for automating lead flow from Webflow form to contractor delivery. Make is the authoritative orchestration platform for CWDB lead routing.

change_log.change: Pivoted to Claude RemoteTrigger (no Make account).

Restored Make-based architecture. RemoteTrigger pivot reversed by James per reliability decision — lost notifications = lost $1,000 bids; Make's enterprise-grade uptime wins over RemoteTrigger's dependency on Anthropic availability.

Built sc-002 'CWDB Lead Routing webhook->fact_bids (TEST)' (scenario 5361099, hook 2442209) per approval_queue approval_id 1 (trace b7a0af806648). New stripped-down chain: Custom Webhook -> PostgREST upsert fact_leads -> PostgREST insert fact_bids. End-to-end test passed (execution 743cd9d361154ce1b5411e4706100315); test rows deleted; v_clean_leads excluded the @cwdb-internal.test email; validation gate counts unchanged. Scenario left INACTIVE. Parked scenario 4792854 untouched.

scenarios.name: Webflow Form → Lead Qualification → HubSpot → Contractor Notification
scenarios.trigger: Make Webhooks module (Custom webhook endpoint — Webflow Project Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → form_submission posts to this URL)

steps.notes: Parses 10 fields: name, phone, email, address, owner, project_type, budget, timeline, notes, tcpa_consent

steps.action: Run lead qualification scoring per /operations/leads/scoring-rules.json (pass threshold: 60/100)
steps.notes: Hard disqualifiers: renter, outside territory (zip not in 54401/54403/54476/54474/54452), invalid US phone, no TCPA consent. Route splits qualified vs disqualified paths.

steps.action: Look up active contractors from HubSpot (lifecyclestage=customer)
steps.notes: Returns Ben Barton + John Garcia (and future active contractors). Pull email + phone + company + firstname.

steps.action: [QUALIFIED PATH] Create HubSpot contact (homeowner) with deal
steps.module: HubSpot CRM → Create a Contact + Create a Deal
steps.notes: Contact properties: name, phone, email, address, project_type, budget, timeline, notes, tcpa_consent=true, lifecyclestage=lead. Deal: Homeowner Leads pipeline (TBD), stage=New Lead, amount=0, dealname='[City] — [project_type] — [name]'.

steps.action: [DISQUALIFIED PATH] Create HubSpot contact with tag and stop
steps.notes: Properties: lifecyclestage=lead, hs_lead_status=UNQUALIFIED, add note with disqualify_reason. Send admin alert only — do not notify contractors.

steps.action: Iterator — loop over each active contractor from step 3
Send email notification to each active contractor
steps.module: Gmail → Send an Email (or Email → Send Email via SMTP)
steps.subject: New deck lead — [City] — [project_type] — [name]

steps.action: Send SMS notification to each active contractor
steps.body_template: New CWDB deck lead: [name] · [city] · [project_type] · Budget [budget] · Timeline [timeline] · [phone]. View in HubSpot: [deal_url]

steps.action: Send admin summary email to slogarjw@gmail.com
steps.subject: [CWDB] Lead routed — [name] — score [score]/100
steps.body: Qualification result, score, contractors notified, HubSpot deal link.

scenarios.status: BUILT 2026-04-18 — scenario inactive pending: (1) Jim authorizes HubSpot + Gmail + Twilio connections via credential-request URL, (2) webhook URL pasted into Webflow Project Settings → Integrations → Webhooks, (3) end-to-end test submission verified, (4) scenario activated manually in Make UI
manual_setup_steps: 1. Build scenario in Make (team ID TBD)
2. Copy webhook URL from Make Webhooks module
3. In Webflow: Project Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → Add Webhook → Event: form_submission → paste URL
4. Verify HubSpot 'Contractor Sales' + 'Homeowner Leads' pipelines exist (see /sales/crm/pipeline-stages.json)
5. Verify Ben Barton + John Garcia in HubSpot have lifecyclestage=customer, valid email + phone
6. Connect Gmail + Twilio in Make → Connections
7. Run test submission from Webflow staging → verify full flow end-to-end

scenarios.name: CWDB Lead Routing webhook->fact_bids (TEST)
scenarios.purpose: Minimal webhook -> Supabase warehouse routing skeleton, built per human-approved control-plane task (approval_queue approval_id 1, action_kind build_make_routing_scenario, trace b7a0af806648). Proves a form payload can land as fact_leads + a pending fact_bids row with no HubSpot/Gmail/Twilio dependencies. NOT wired to Webflow; for selftest traffic only.
scenarios.trigger: Make Webhooks module (Custom webhook; nothing external posts to it — test payloads only)

steps.notes: Interface: webflow_submission_id, submitted_at, date_key, name, phone, email, tcpa_consent_given, utm_source

POST https://iabiwsbmnbxmkjvkgfhg.supabase.co/rest/v1/fact_leads?on_conflict=webflow_submission_id with headers apikey + Authorization Bearer (service-role) + Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates,return=representation. Body adds lead_channel=webform, tcpa_consent_source=form. webflow_submission_id is the upsert natural key (only non-HubSpot unique index on fact_leads). parseResponse=true; returns lead_id.

steps.action: Insert pending bid row into Supabase fact_bids
steps.notes: POST https://iabiwsbmnbxmkjvkgfhg.supabase.co/rest/v1/fact_bids with Prefer: return=representation. Body: {lead_id: {{2.data[1].lead_id}}, contractor_id: null, bid_status: 'pending'}.

scenarios.status: ACTIVE 2026-06-11T20:29 UTC — scenario activated per approval_id 60 (trace t8f3a2c9). v2 blueprint: webhook→router(null-email-abort|has-email)→fact_leads upsert on_conflict=email→Jim email notify. No fact_bids module. No HubSpot modules. Gmail connection 8444800 (slogarjw@gmail.com). Smoke test execution a1fb56b211454e05940aed8876cabc07: SUCCESS, 3 ops, 847ms, lead_id=122 created+deleted, 0 fact_bids. PENDING: Webflow form_submission webhook not yet added (requires Jim manual action in Webflow Project Settings > Integrations > Webhooks, URL=https://hook.us2.make.com/j1794cbx29uv6al9gbqkpjdvugutmcpc).

verification.approval_ref: approval_queue approval_id 1, trace b7a0af806648, council_id 1 feedback honored

connections_required.notes: Auth via Supabase service-role key embedded in module headers (key source: .env.local SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY)

notes: Make orchestrates the entire lead delivery flow. HubSpot MCP, Gmail MCP, and Claude RemoteTrigger are no longer the primary orchestration path. They may still be used by specialist agents for one-off CRM tasks or admin work — not for production lead routing.
